short: A 16-bit float format that keeps a wide exponent range for stable training.
definition: BFloat16 (Brain Float 16) is a 16-bit floating-point format with 1 sign bit, 8 exponent bits, and 7 mantissa bits, matching the exponent range of a 32-bit float while halving storage. Keeping that range makes it more numerically stable than FP16 (another 16-bit format) for training, handling very large or small gradients without overflow or underflow. It is the default training format on Google TPUs (Google's AI chips) and recent NVIDIA GPUs, enabling fast mixed-precision training without loss-scaling tricks.
